name: R & A Transportation LLC
recent_news: Owner Rob Waliczek announced retirement effective Nov 21, 2018; company cancelled Iron Mountain and Norway contracts due to driver shortage; Iron Mountain Public Schools now uses Choice Transportation (2025-26), suggesting R&A may no longer be operating as a school bus contractor.
fit_notes: Likely not a viable tuck-in target. Owner announced retirement in 2018, contracts cancelled with two districts; current district uses different contractor. Entity may still exist as small LLC but operating status as a school bus contractor is highly uncertain. Even at 23 listed employees the business has likely wound down.

name: Porter Transportation
districts_or_customers: Likely Cobre Consolidated Schools or Silver Consolidated Schools (Bayard/Silver City, Grant County, NM) - not confirmed
recent_news: Long-standing small operator established 1979 in Bayard, NM; listed in Silver City Grant County Chamber alongside Montoya Transportation. Manta lists ~$130K revenue / 2 employees but NM PED contractor list and source data suggest larger school bus operation (17 employees per source file).
fit_notes: Small, long-tenured family operator in rural southwestern New Mexico (Grant County). Likely too small to be a meaningful tuck-in on its own; only attractive as part of a multi-asset NM rollup. No succession signals confirmed but 1979 founding suggests potential aging-owner dynamic worth a single discovery call.

name: G & K Bus Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely St. Francis Area Schools ISD 15 (Bethel/East Bethel area, Anoka County) - not confirmed
recent_news: No web footprint found; no news. Small private operator at 23742 University Ave E, Bethel, MN 55005. Likely MSBOA member given Minnesota's heavy use of private contractors but membership not confirmed.
fit_notes: Sub-scale family/owner-operator in Anoka County exurbs north of Minneapolis. Too small to move the needle for Summit standalone; only useful as part of an MN cluster build. No web presence is itself a soft succession signal.

name: Quality Education Bus Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Possibly private/parochial schools in Duplin County, NC (Wallace Christian Academy, New Hope Christian Academy) - not confirmed; NC public schools generally run buses in-house
recent_news: No web footprint found. North Carolina public school districts overwhelmingly self-operate transportation under NC DPI, so a private bus company in Wallace is likely serving private schools, charter schools, or providing supplemental/special-needs/charter routes.
fit_notes: Very small operator in eastern NC where public districts run buses internally; revenue base is likely private/charter-school or SPED, limiting tuck-in synergy. Low priority unless Summit specifically wants a Duplin/Sampson County toehold.

name: B P & B M Sch Bus Trnsprtn Crp
districts_or_customers: Likely Brockton Public Schools and/or surrounding southeastern MA districts; possible private/parochial routes - not confirmed
recent_news: No web footprint found; not the same entity as Plymouth & Brockton (P&B) intercity bus. Operates from a residential-style address (53 Oak Ridge Dr W, Brockton) consistent with a small mom-and-pop yard-and-buses contractor. Name 'BP & BM' likely refers to founders' initials.
fit_notes: Tiny family-owned MA contractor in Brockton; MA market is heavily consolidated (NRT Bus, North Reading Transportation, Van Pool, etc.), making sub-scale operators natural tuck-in candidates. Female-named president suggests possible 2nd-gen family ownership and worth a discovery call given succession potential.
